title: JSPS Invitational Fellowships for Research in Japan 2026: Fully Funded Research Collaboration Opportunity for International Scholars
summary: The JSPS Invitational Fellowships for Research in Japan 2026, offered by the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), provide an exceptional opportunity for international researchers to collaborate with leading scholars at Japanese universities and research institutions. This prestigious fellowship program aims to strengthen international academic partnerships, promote collaborative research, and enhance global knowledge exchange by inviting outstanding overseas researchers to conduct research in Japan. Through the JSPS Invitational Fellowships, internationally recognized scholars and promising researchers can undertake advanced research projects in collaboration with Japanese host researchers. The program encourages the development of long-term academic relationships, joint publications, and innovative research initiatives across a wide range of disciplines. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Applications are currently open for the FY2026 JSPS Invitational Fellowships (Short-term Second Call), with the application deadline set for 28 April 2026 at 5:00 PM (Japan Standard Time) . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This fellowship provides a unique platform for international researchers to engage with Japan s vibrant academic community, access world-class research facilities, and contribute to cutting-edge scientific and scholarly developments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS) The Japan Society for the Promotion of Science is one of Japan s most prominent organizations dedicated to advancing academic research and international collaboration. JSPS supports research excellence by funding fellowships, promoting partnerships between Japanese and international institutions, and fostering global knowledge exchange. Through its various fellowship programs, JSPS has played a key role in strengthening Japan s research ecosystem while connecting scholars across disciplines and geographic regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Invitational Fellowship Program specifically focuses on inviting distinguished overseas researchers to collaborate with Japanese host institutions. Overview of the JSPS Invitational Fellowships for Research in Japan The JSPS Invitational Fellowship Program allows Japanese host researchers to invite international scholars to Japan for collaborative research activities. The fellowship supports research visits that foster international academic exchange and strengthen partnerships between Japanese and overseas institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The program supports a broad range of research disciplines, including: Natural sciences Engineering Medical sciences Social sciences Humanities Interdisciplinary research fields The fellowship is structured to facilitate collaboration between international researchers and Japanese host institutions, enabling fellows to conduct research while engaging in scholarly discussions, seminars, and academic networking activities. Applications must be submitted by a host researcher in Japan , who invites the overseas researcher and submits the application through the JSPS Electronic Application System. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fellowship Types and Duration The JSPS Invitational Fellowship Program offers two main categories designed to accommodate different research collaboration needs. Short-Term Invitational Fellowship The short-term fellowship is designed for international researchers who wish to conduct collaborative research for a limited period at a Japanese institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The FY2026 call currently open is the Short-Term Invitational Fellowship (Second Call) . This category supports short-term research visits focused on collaboration, academic exchange, and knowledge sharing. Long-Term Invitational Fellowship The long-term fellowship supports more extended research collaborations between international scholars and Japanese host researchers. Long-term fellowships allow invited researchers to participate in sustained collaborative projects that may lead to significant academic outputs, including research publications and future grant applications. Fellowship Objectives The JSPS Invitational Fellowships aim to achieve several key objectives: Strengthening international academic collaboration between Japan and global research communities Promoting joint research initiatives between Japanese and overseas scholars Facilitating knowledge exchange across disciplines Enhancing the internationalization of Japanese universities and research institutions Supporting innovative research with global impact ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); By bringing international researchers to Japan, the program also contributes to the development of new research networks and long-term institutional partnerships. Benefits of the JSPS Invitational Fellowship Selected fellows benefit from a range of academic and research opportunities during their stay in Japan. Collaborative Research Opportunities Fellows work closely with Japanese host researchers to develop research projects, conduct studies, and produce scholarly publications. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Access to Research Facilities Participants gain access to the host institution s laboratories, libraries, and research infrastructure, allowing them to advance their research projects in a highly supportive academic environment. Academic Networking The fellowship provides opportunities to interact with leading Japanese scholars, participate in seminars, attend conferences, and build international academic networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Institutional Collaboration The program encourages the creation of long-term collaborations between Japanese and international universities, potentially leading to future joint research initiatives and institutional partnerships. Eligibility Criteria The JSPS Invitational Fellowship is designed for outstanding overseas researchers who can contribute significantly to collaborative research with Japanese institutions. Candidates must be invited by a Japanese host researcher affiliated with a Japanese university or research institution . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Applicants must demonstrate strong academic credentials and a track record of research excellence in their respective fields. The fellowship is open to researchers across multiple academic disciplines, provided their research aligns with the expertise of the Japanese host institution. See the List of Eligible Host Institutes (as of September, 2024) (PDF/1.3MB) Application Process Applications must be submitted through the JSPS Electronic Application System . Unlike many fellowship programs where applicants submit their own applications, the JSPS Invitational Fellowship requires that the application be submitted by the Japanese host researcher . The host researcher prepares and submits the application through their institution. The application must include: Research proposal developed jointly by the host researcher and invited overseas researcher Curriculum Vitae of the invited researcher Required application forms provided by JSPS The application must also be approved by the head of the host institution before submission to JSPS. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare several documents as part of the fellowship application. These include: Research Proposal Document jointly prepared by the host researcher and invited overseas researcher Curriculum Vitae of the invited researcher Online application form completed through the JSPS Electronic Application System Applicants should carefully follow the instructions provided in the official application guidelines to ensure their application meets all requirements. Incomplete applications or submissions made outside the official electronic system will not be considered. Selection Process Applications are evaluated by JSPS through a competitive review process. Review committees assess applications based on several factors, including: Academic excellence and research achievements of the invited researcher Quality and feasibility of the proposed research project Potential impact of the research collaboration Alignment between the invited researcher and the Japanese host institution ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Only highly competitive proposals demonstrating strong academic value and international collaboration potential are selected. Important Dates Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 (5:00 PM JST) Applicants should note that host institutions may set earlier internal deadlines for submission before forwarding applications to JSPS. Visit the Official JSPS website to learn more about this opportunity. The post JSPS Invitational Fellowships for Research in Japan 2026: Fully Funded Research Collaboration Opportunity for International Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities)
summary: SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for its 2026 postgraduate programmes, including both Master s degrees and Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) research programmes. Recognized globally for its expertise in the study of Asia, Africa, and the Middle East, SOAS offers one of the most diverse and intellectually rigorous academic environments in the world. Students from across the globe can apply to pursue advanced studies across a wide range of disciplines including international relations, development studies, law, economics, anthropology, languages, politics, media studies, and many other specialized fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Application deadlines vary depending on the programme: Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) Programmes Deadline: 15 June 2026 Master s Programmes Deadline: 31 July 2026 SOAS strongly encourages applicants to apply early within the admissions cycle due to the time required for application review, supervision allocation for research degrees, and visa processing for international students. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About SOAS University of London SOAS University of London, also known as the School of Oriental and African Studies, is one of the world s leading institutions for the study of global cultures, languages, and societies, particularly focusing on Asia, Africa, and the Middle East. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Located in central London, SOAS provides a unique academic environment that blends rigorous scholarship with global perspectives. The university offers more than 200 postgraduate programmes designed to equip students with advanced knowledge and critical thinking skills necessary to address global challenges. SOAS programmes are internationally recognized for their interdisciplinary approach, offering students a rich academic experience combined with opportunities for research, policy engagement, and professional development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Master s Programmes at SOAS (2026 Entry) Applications are now open for postgraduate taught programmes beginning in September 2026. Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 These programmes include degrees such as: MA (Master of Arts) MSc (Master of Science) LLM (Master of Laws) MMus (Master of Music) MRes (Master of Research) SOAS offers more than 100 postgraduate taught programmes covering diverse subject areas including: Development Studies Global Media and Digital Communications International Relations Politics and International Studies Anthropology Law and Legal Studies Economics and Finance Languages and Cultures Environmental Studies Music and Arts ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Each programme provides specialist academic training and a global perspective, preparing graduates for careers in academia, government, international organizations, NGOs, and the private sector. Step-by-Step Guide to Applying for a SOAS Master s Programme Step 1: Choose the Right Programme Applicants should first explore the available postgraduate programmes to determine which aligns best with their academic and professional goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Each programme page includes: Detailed curriculum information Entry requirements Study modes Tuition fees Programme structure Applicants can only apply to one postgraduate programme at SOAS per year, though they may indicate a second-choice programme in case their first choice is unsuccessful. For applicants who require a UK student visa, SOAS will only sponsor full-time study. Students intending to apply for scholarships or financial sponsorship are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Step 2: Check Entry Requirements Entry requirements vary by programme but generally include the following: Academic Requirements Applicants must normally hold a Bachelor s (Honours) degree with a classification equivalent to a UK 2:2 or above. Some programmes may have additional subject-specific requirements. International applicants should consult the country-specific entry requirements page to confirm equivalency. English Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate English proficiency sufficient to engage fully with their academic programme. Accepted evidence may include: IELTS TOEFL Other recognized English language qualifications If an applicant s English language level does not meet the requirements, preparatory courses are available through the International Foundation Courses and English Language Studies (IFCELS) department. These preparatory programmes help international students strengthen their academic English skills and meet admission requirements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Step 3: Submit Your Application Applicants must submit the required documents through the online application system. Required documentation includes: Academic Documents Degree transcripts showing all modules and marks Degree certificate (if the programme has already been completed) Interim transcript (for students currently studying) All documents must be submitted in English or accompanied by certified translations. Additional Required Documents Passport identity page Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume Supporting statement (approximately 1,000 words) The personal statement should explain: Motivation for studying the programme Relevant experience Career goals Suitability for the programme ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Applicants are advised to write their statements in their own words and avoid using templates. References are not required for most Master s programmes but may be included if desired. Step 4: After Submitting Your Application SOAS aims to process complete applications within approximately four weeks. During the evaluation process: Admissions staff review the application materials Additional information may be requested if necessary ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Successful applicants receive an offer and are directed to the next steps required for enrolment. Learn more and apply for the Master s Programmes at SOAS HERE . MPhil/PhD Research Programmes at SOAS For students interested in academic research careers, SOAS offers doctoral research programmes leading to MPhil or PhD degrees. Application Deadline: 15 June 2026 Applicants should submit their applications well before the deadline to allow sufficient time for academic assessment and supervision allocation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Important Steps Before Applying for a PhD Prospective doctoral applicants must complete several preparatory steps before submitting their application. Confirm Entry Requirements Applicants must normally hold: A good Master s degree in a relevant field (or international equivalent) Identify a Supervisor Applicants must determine whether the relevant department has staff who can supervise their proposed research. A potential supervisor should be identified and referenced in the research proposal. Contact the PhD Coordinator Applicants are encouraged to contact the PhD coordinator for their research area before submitting an application. Explore Funding Options Students should investigate available funding opportunities, including scholarships, studentships, and grants. Required Documents for PhD Applications A complete doctoral application must include: Formal application form Personal or supporting statement Curriculum Vitae (CV) Research proposal Academic transcripts Degree certificates One academic reference Incomplete applications will not be considered by the admissions team. The Doctoral School Admissions team first verifies that all required documents are present before forwarding the application to academic selectors for evaluation. Learn more and apply for the MPhil/PhD Research Programmes at SOAS HERE . Global Partnerships and Joint Programmes Some SOAS doctoral programmes are offered in collaboration with international partner institutions. One example is the joint MPhil/PhD in Applied Development Economics with the University of the Witwatersrand (Wits). Applicants must specify their Home Institution within the application form. The Home Institution is the university where the student will be primarily based during the programme. Application Advice for International Students International applicants should plan ahead due to visa processing timelines. SOAS advises overseas students to apply well in advance of their intended start date. For visiting research students, applications must be submitted at least three months prior to the term start date. Accessibility and Student Support SOAS encourages applications from students with disabilities. Admissions decisions are made based on academic merit. However, applicants who may require additional academic support or accommodations are encouraged to indicate this in their application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); The university provides support services and adjustments where necessary to ensure equitable learning opportunities. Funding and Scholarships Students interested in funding their studies at SOAS may explore a variety of options including: Scholarships Grants Student loans External sponsorships Funding availability varies by programme and nationality. Applicants interested in scholarships are encouraged to submit their applications early. Contact Information Email: study@soas.ac.uk Telephone: +44 (0)20 3510 6974 Address: SOAS University of London 10 Thornhaugh Street Russell Square London WC1H 0XG United Kingdom Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Research Collaboration Opportunity for African Scholars
summary: The AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026 at the University of Oxford offers an exceptional opportunity for outstanding African researchers to collaborate with leading scholars at one of the world s most prestigious universities. Designed to foster long-term research partnerships, this fellowship provides a unique platform for African academics to build global networks, advance high-impact research projects, and strengthen collaboration between African institutions and the University of Oxford. Applications are now open for the 2026 AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme, with the application deadline set for 15 May 2026 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through this prestigious fellowship, selected scholars will be affiliated with the University of Oxford for 12 months , including 10 months of virtual engagement and a 2-month in-person research residency in Oxford during Trinity Term. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This programme, coordinated through the Africa Oxford Initiative (AfOx), aims to promote multidisciplinary collaboration, strengthen research capacity in Africa, and enable knowledge exchange between Oxford scholars and researchers working across the African continent. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme The AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme was created to support collaboration between exceptional African researchers and Oxford-based academics. Fellows undertake a research project of their choice while working closely with an academic collaborator from the University of Oxford. The programme offers scholars the opportunity to: Conduct collaborative research with Oxford academics Access world-class research resources and libraries Develop joint research projects and publications Build sustainable international academic networks Contribute to academic discussions and intellectual life at Oxford ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The fellowship encourages multidisciplinary research and welcomes scholars from diverse academic disciplines. Applicants are required to identify an Oxford-based collaborator and include them in their fellowship proposal before submitting their application. Fellowship Duration and Structure The AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme runs for 12 months , combining both remote engagement and a residential research visit. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 1. Virtual Engagement (10 Months) During the virtual phase, fellows will have remote access to resources at the University of Oxford. Activities may include: Research collaboration with Oxford faculty Virtual seminars and workshops Academic writing and project development Research leadership development ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This phase helps fellows establish strong collaboration before their residency. 2. In-Person Residency at Oxford (2 Months) The fellowship includes a two-month residency at Oxford between April and June (Trinity Term) . During this time, fellows will: Work directly with Oxford researchers Access university research facilities Participate in seminars and academic discussions Present their research at workshops Develop collaborative research outputs Each fellow will also be affiliated with a specific Oxford department and college . Research Partnerships and Fellowship Themes The AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme collaborates with multiple Oxford departments and research centres, offering fellows opportunities to work in diverse academic fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Mathematical Institute Fellowship This fellowship connects African mathematicians with the Oxford Mathematical Institute, one of the world s leading mathematics departments. Fellows collaborate with researchers across areas of fundamental and applied mathematics while developing research papers, events, and joint projects. Oxford Department of International Development (ODID) The ODID fellowship supports research related to global development challenges. ODID hosts major research initiatives such as: Refugee Studies Centre Young Lives Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative Technology and Industrialisation Development Centre ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Researchers working on development policy, poverty reduction, and global economic transformation are encouraged to apply. Law Faculty Fellowship The AfOx Law Fellowship supports African academics working in legal research. Candidates must hold a full-time academic appointment at an African university and demonstrate a clear research agenda that would benefit from collaboration with Oxford scholars. African Studies Centre Fellowship The African Studies Centre is one of the world s leading hubs for interdisciplinary African scholarship. Fellows have the opportunity to conduct research, writing, and mentoring while using Oxford s vast academic resources. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); During the fellowship, participants present their work at a writing workshop where Oxford scholars provide feedback to support publication. TORCH Humanities Fellowship The Oxford Research Centre in the Humanities (TORCH) promotes interdisciplinary research across humanities disciplines. Priority research areas may include: Environmental humanities Medical humanities Digital humanities Cultural heritage Race and resistance Refugee Studies Centre Fellowship Researchers working on migration, displacement, refugee policy, and humanitarian responses can collaborate with the Refugee Studies Centre. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); The centre supports interdisciplinary research across fields such as anthropology, law, economics, politics, and international relations. Healthcare Access Fellowship This fellowship focuses on improving equitable access to healthcare in low- and middle-income countries. Projects may address issues such as: Healthcare infrastructure Drug pricing Regulatory systems Health policy Access to medicines Pandemic Sciences Institute Fellowship This fellowship focuses on research related to infectious diseases and pandemic preparedness, including fields such as epidemiology, vaccinology, policy, and ethics. Oxford Cancer Fellowship Researchers working in oncology can collaborate with Oxford Cancer across priority areas including: Immuno-oncology Cancer early detection and prevention Cancer big data Novel therapeutics Ethox Bioethics Fellowship The Ethox Centre focuses on global health bioethics. This fellowship supports interdisciplinary projects addressing ethical issues in healthcare and medical research, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Oxford EARTH Fellowship This fellowship supports research addressing global environmental challenges, including sustainable natural resource management, renewable energy technologies, and environmental remediation. OCIS Fellowship The AfOx Oxford Centre for Islamic Studies Fellowship focuses on research related to: Islamic sciences Muslim societies Islamic finance Science and technology in Muslim contexts RAIN Fellowship Programme The AfOx Research Alpha Industry Fellowship focuses on Africa s energy sector, including renewable energy, climate-resilient power systems, and energy access innovations. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows receive comprehensive support throughout the programme. Research Collaboration Fellows work closely with Oxford-based researchers to develop joint projects, publications, and grant proposals. Access to Oxford Resources Participants gain access to Oxford s libraries, research networks, and academic infrastructure. Residential Research Visit The programme covers costs for the in-person residency, including accommodation and travel. Financial Support Fellows receive financial support including: Return economy-class flight Accommodation during residency Visa reimbursement Maintenance allowance of up to 250 per week during residence Airport transfers Academic Networking Participants build strong global networks with Oxford scholars, policymakers, and researchers. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet several criteria to qualify for the fellowship. Candidates must: Be of postdoctoral or equivalent academic status Hold a position at an African academic or research institution Identify and secure an Oxford-based collaborator Submit a research proposal aligned with one of the fellowship themes Demonstrate strong research potential and impact Applicants must also ensure that their Oxford collaborator provides a formal letter of support . Applications submitted without the collaborator s signed support letter will be disqualified. Selection Criteria Applications are assessed based on: Quality and originality of the research proposal Alignment between the applicant and Oxford collaborator Feasibility of the project within the fellowship timeline Potential for long-term research collaboration Academic merit and impact of the research Gender balance and regional diversity are also considered during selection. Application Process Applicants must complete the fellowship application online through the AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme portal . The application must include: A completed fellowship application form A research proposal Details of the Oxford collaborator and department A signed letter of support from the Oxford host Applicants must ensure all materials are submitted before the deadline. Applications via email are not accepted. Important Dates Application Deadline: 15 May 2026 Fellowship Duration: 12 months Virtual Engagement: 10 months Residency at Oxford: 2 months during Trinity Term (May June) How to Apply Interested researchers should visit the Official Fellowship page and submit their application online. Applicants must first identify and secure a collaborator at the University of Oxford before applying. Prospective fellows are encouraged to contact potential collaborators early and align their research project with the host department s interests. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Discover more global fellowship programmes HERE . The post AfOx Visiting Fellowship Programme 2026 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Research Collaboration Opportunity for African Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South
summary: The Pulitzer Center has launched a special call for proposals inviting journalists to report on the impact of unprecedented cuts to U.S. health aid across the Global South. This funding opportunity aims to support in-depth, high-impact stories that bring eyewitness accounts from affected communities to U.S. audiences. As U.S. health assistance programs face significant reductions including the winding down of initiatives linked to USAID many health advances in Africa and other regions now face an uncertain future. The Center is seeking ambitious reporting that highlights the human consequences of these changes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Priority Reporting Areas The Pulitzer Center is particularly interested in proposals that explore: Children s health Women s health , including maternal care HIV control and treatment , and other infectious diseases Programs addressing malnutrition, starvation, and waterborne diseases Underreported scientific research or ground-level aid disruptions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Journalists are also welcome to apply with other compelling health-related stories that align with the broader theme. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Who Is Eligible? To apply, you must be: A staff reporter or editor with a U.S.-based newsroom, or A freelancer with a confirmed commitment or strong interest from a U.S. outlet or a publication with a significant U.S. audience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This special grant is specifically intended for stories that will reach audiences in the United States. Grant Funding Budget Guidelines The Pulitzer Center is seeking ambitious, high-quality reporting in: Text Visual journalism Audio Multimedia formats Funding range: $5,000 to $20,000 , depending on the scope and ambition of the project. Budgets exceeding this range must demonstrate exceptional potential for reach and impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); For freelancers, proposals may include a reasonable stipend , but the majority of funds should cover travel and essential reporting costs. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the deadline. Deadline March 30, 2026 Journalists are encouraged to apply early, as submissions will be assessed as they are received. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Previously Supported Health Projects The Pulitzer Center has backed impactful health investigations such as: Slashed by Trump, This Cutting-Edge HIV Vaccine Has a New Path NPR Asia s Timor-Leste Nation Copes With End of USAID The New York Times The Lancet The Children Left Behind in Global Health Cuts Science Magazine Cuts and Consequences: The End of USAID PBS NewsHour US Funding Cuts Imperil Global Efforts To Eliminate Cervical Cancer CNN ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); These projects demonstrate the Center s commitment to funding journalism that drives awareness, accountability, and global impact. How to Apply Applicants can review frequently asked questions and submit grant application form . If you are a journalist covering health, development, or global policy and especially if you report from Africa or other regions heavily impacted by aid cuts this is a timely opportunity to bring critical stories to U.S. audiences with the support of a respected global journalism finder. For more information on grant opportunities, Visit Here . The post Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Young Leaders GenC Program by Ashoka
summary: Young people today are not just the leaders of tomorrow they are change agents today. If you re passionate about social impact and community transformation, Generation Changemaker (GenC) by Ashoka offers a unique platform to strengthen your changemaking skills, build a powerful network, and amplify your ideas for positive change. The program is open to young changemakers from Austria, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, and for the first time, several other European countries including Germany, Italy, Poland, Belgium, and the United Kingdom. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Over the course of one year, participants benefit from workshops, peer exchanges, in-person meetups, and opportunities to connect with both young leaders and experienced professionals. This is an opportunity to grow your voice, expand your influence, and learn how to turn ideas into action in your community and beyond. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the GenC 2026 Program GenC is an initiative by Ashoka , a global nonprofit pioneering social entrepreneurship and collective impact. Ashoka believes that everyone can be a changemaker , and the GenC program puts young change leaders at the center of this mission. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants will take part in a wide range of activities designed to build confidence, connect with peers, and strengthen changemaking capabilities. The program features: Peer-to-peer gatherings where young leaders exchange ideas and experiences in structured and informal settings. Online workshops and discussion formats led by experts across sectors to build skills and inspire innovation. In-person events including a nationwide meeting and an international GenC weekend with changemakers from multiple countries. Opportunities to amplify your voice through public visibility and engagement with decision-makers, making your perspectives heard on topics like climate, inclusion, education, and social justice. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Importantly, the program focuses on young changemakers who are already involved in community action or are ready to start making a difference . Whether you ve led a project, organized a campaign, started a community initiative, or even just have an idea you re excited about, GenC is designed to help you grow and connect. Why This Program Matters Globally, young people are leading movements, innovating community solutions, and challenging old ways of thinking. Yet despite their critical role, young leaders often lack platforms, resources, and networks that help them scale their ideas and connect with broader impact ecosystems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This is where GenC makes a difference. It provides: A community of peers and mentors who support learning and collaboration Real opportunities to build confidence and skills through workshops and leadership sessions Visibility and access to public platforms and decision-makers Cross-border connections that expand perspectives and inspire collective action By engaging in GenC 2026, young changemakers not only deepen their own capacities but also contribute to shifting how society views youth leadership from spectators to co-creators of change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); The program s emphasis on experiential learning, peer exchange, and networking gives participants tools to navigate complex challenges, design solutions that are contextually relevant, and collaborate across cultures and disciplines. In a world where young voices are driving social, environmental, and policy shifts, programs like GenC empower participants not only to respond to challenges but to anticipate, influence, and lead change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Who Can Apply GenC 2026 is open to: Young individuals aged 14 24 Residents of Austria, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, Germany, Italy, Poland, Belgium, and the United Kingdom. Importantly, the program does not require previous formal qualifications ; what matters most is your commitment to positive change and your willingness to engage, learn, and grow. Application Timeline Next Steps Application deadline: 15 March 2026 Program duration: One year of workshops, gatherings, and collaboration opportunities across borders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); To apply, visit the official Ashoka GenC program page and follow the registration instructions. Your application will ask for basic information about your interests, experiences, and what drives you toward positive change. Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the O pportunities for Youth website. The post Apply for the Young Leaders GenC Program by Ashoka appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support
summary: For students passionate about advancing knowledge through research, Australia offers one of the world s most significant government-backed scholarship frameworks: the Research Training Program (RTP) . Administered through the Australian Government s Research Block Grants, the RTP provides funding to support domestic and international students undertaking higher degrees by research (HDRs) such as PhDs or research-focused Master s degrees at eligible Australian universities. Whether you envision a career in academia, industry research, policy analysis, or development innovation, the RTP can unlock financial support that makes pursuing a research degree in Australia both feasible and attractive. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What Is the Research Training Program? The Research Training Program (RTP) is part of Australia s broader Research Block Grants framework , which also includes the Research Support Program. While the Research Support Program helps Australian universities cover systemic research costs, the RTP specifically targets the training and support of research students. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Under the RTP, eligible higher education providers receive government funding they can use to offer scholarships to students undertaking higher degrees by research including research doctorates and research Masters degrees. These scholarships provide a combination of financial support, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Tuition fee offsets that cover the cost of university research tuition Stipends to assist with general living costs Allowances for ancillary research-related expenses (such as relocation, printing, or health insurance) Different universities handle the application and selection process for RTP scholarships themselves. Prospective candidates must therefore contact their chosen institution directly to learn how to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why This Opportunity Matters Research degrees are gateways to deep expertise and innovation that shape industries, public policy, and scientific discovery. However, pursuing a higher degree by research (HDR) such as a PhD or research master s can be financially and logistically demanding. Tuition costs, living expenses, health insurance, and the long time commitment required often pose significant barriers to talented researchers. The Australian Government s Research Training Program makes advanced research study more accessible by alleviating these financial pressures. RTP scholarships help students focus on their research contributions rather than their costs of living, enabling deeper engagement with their work. Scholarships can cover tuition fees entirely, provide tax-free stipends for living costs, and include useful allowances for ancillary research support making study in Australia a viable option for both domestic and international candidates. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Moreover, the RTP supports students in training environments that foster collaboration with industry and research end-users, helping bridge academic research with real-world impact. Through partnerships between universities and industry, RTP recipients can gain valuable experience, build professional networks, and enhance their long-term career prospects beyond academia whether in government, enterprise, or the non-profit sector. In a global landscape where research excellence is increasingly tied to innovation and socioeconomic progress, the RTP stands as a strategic investment in future leaders and problem-solvers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); How Long Are RTP Scholarships? RTP scholarships support students for the duration of their research degrees, subject to guidelines and university discretion: Research doctorate candidates : Typically supported for 3 to 4 years full-time (or 6 to 8 years part-time) Research Master s candidates : Supported for up to 2 years full-time (or up to 4 years part-time) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The exact period of support is determined by the university offering the RTP scholarship and the rules set out in Australia s Higher Education Support (Commonwealth Scholarships) Guidelines, which were updated in 2025. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for an RTP scholarship, students must: Be enrolled (or intending to enrol) in an accredited research doctorate or research Master s degree (HDR) at an eligible Australian university Meet individual university entry requirements, which typically include academic transcripts, research proposals, and supervisor support Both domestic and international students can be offered RTP scholarships, though the application, selection criteria, and availability vary by institution. Students may apply for RTP support at multiple universities, and universities may consider prior study, research experience, and publications when selecting recipients. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Because RTP scholarships are administered at the university level, interested applicants should contact the postgraduate research office of their chosen institution to confirm eligibility, specific application dates, and required materials. How to Apply RTP scholarships are awarded by eligible Australian higher education providers. Prospective HDR students should: Choose an Australian university offering a research doctorate or research master s aligned with their interests. Contact the postgraduate research office for details on the university s RTP application procedure. Submit required documents (e.g., research proposal, academic records, supervisor support) by the university s deadline. Learn more about RTP scholarships on the official website. Each university s process may differ, so early inquiry and preparation are essential. Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The post Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student & Young Professionals Policy Seminar
summary: The IMANI Center for Policy Education has officially opened applications for the 12th Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA) 2026 , its flagship pan-African summer seminar designed to cultivate the next generation of African policy and business leaders. SYPALA is a highly competitive residential program that brings together outstanding undergraduate students and recent graduates from across Africa who are passionate about strengthening economic resilience and advancing inclusive governance within their countries. Admission into the program is based solely on the quality of the applicant s essay , making intellectual rigor, originality, and policy insight the core selection criteria. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About SYPALA SYPALA is a four-day immersive academic seminar organized by the IMANI Center for Policy Education . Over the years, the academy has built a strong reputation for convening high-performing young Africans to engage deeply with ideas around public policy, free markets, institutional reform, and governance innovation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Participants benefit from interactive sessions led by world-class faculty, peer-to-peer learning with top students from across the continent, and exposure to practical frameworks for solving Africa s most pressing economic challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Essay Competition Overview Applicants are required to submit an original essay addressing the following topic: Essay Topic: Identify one specific policy or business innovation that can significantly enhance economic resilience and foster inclusive governance in a specific African country. Applicants must justify their choice by analyzing the current challenge, explaining how the proposed solution works, and outlining one major potential obstacle to its implementation. Successful essays will demonstrate strong analytical depth, originality of thought, clarity of argument, and a clear interest in economic resilience and inclusive governance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Essay Requirements Maximum length: 1,200 words Language: English only Submission format: DOCX or PDF Each essay must include a summary of no more than 200 words Proper citations and references are required All submissions must be original; plagiarism results in automatic disqualification Each applicant may submit only one entry Applicants must also include their institution, current academic level, course of study, and age at the time of submission. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Benefits for Accepted Applicants Candidates selected for SYPALA 2026 will receive: Full access to the four-day residential seminar Free accommodation for the duration of the program A full scholarship covering participation costs Direct engagement with a world-class faculty and policy experts How to Apply Essays should be submitted electronically via email to: RA@imanighana.org or info@imanighana.org Application Deadline: All applications must be submitted no later than April 30, 2026. Qualified students and recent graduates across Africa are strongly encouraged to apply. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . The post SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student Young Professionals Policy Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026
summary: The African Women s Development Fund (AWDF) has announced a call for African feminist consultants to support its movement accompaniment and strengthening initiatives across the continent. This paid consultancy opportunity invites experienced feminist practitioners with expertise in collective care, digital security, crisis response, advocacy, and resilience building to apply before 20 March 2026 . In line with its commitment to fostering flourishing feminist movements, AWDF has identified key strategic pathways for accompaniment and is inviting experienced practitioners to apply for one or more specialized assignments. Each consultancy requires distinct thematic expertise, facilitation approaches, and varying levels of engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Available Consultancy Assignments 1. Collective Care as Political Strategy A two-day workshop designed to reposition care from an individual responsibility to a radical, systemic organizational practice. The training frames wellbeing as a frontline defense against backlash and repression. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 2. Digital Security and Collective Safety A four-day in-person training focused on strengthening practical digital security and data protection skills. Consultants will support organizations to develop: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Digital Security Protocols Cyber Harassment Response Plans 3. Crisis Response and Preparedness Training A four-day feminist crisis response and communication preparedness training tailored for organizations facing political, digital, or reputational threats. 4. Research and Advocacy Teach-In A five-day in-person strategic retreat where feminist research meets political action. The program aims to equip partners to transform lived experiences and evidence into powerful narratives for collective futures. Priority expertise: Feminists operating in French and Portuguese-speaking contexts . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 5. Peer Learning Exchange Programme A six-month online journey featuring monthly facilitated peer learning circles covering: Feminist governance Resource mobilization Digital security Collective care Advocacy 6. Resilience Building Accompaniment A six-month online cyclical learning programme addressing: Advocacy in hostile environments Collective care practices Development of Organizational Resilience Action Plans 7. Integrated Resource Directory and Toolkit Development of a living, accessible ecosystem of tools and contextual knowledge aimed at strengthening operational resilience and strategic response capacity for French and Portuguese-speaking women s rights movements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Who Should Apply? AWDF is seeking African feminist consultants with: Proven thematic expertise relevant to the selected assignment(s) Strong facilitation and movement-building experience Contextual understanding of feminist organizing across Africa Capacity to deliver both in-person and virtual programming Applicants may apply for one or multiple assignments depending on their expertise. Application Process Interested practitioners must submit: Technical Expression of Interest Detailed methodology Proposed pathway(s) for movement accompaniment Financial Proposal Send you applications to Consultants@awdf.org Deadline: 20 March 2026 For detailed scope and requirements, applicants are encouraged to review the official Terms of Reference . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Why This Opportunity Matters This call presents a significant opportunity for African feminist practitioners to contribute to strengthening women s rights movements through structured accompaniment, collective learning, and resilience-building strategies. By centering care, digital safety, crisis response, and advocacy, AWDF continues to invest in sustainable feminist futures across the continent. If you are passionate about advancing feminist organizing in Africa, this is your opportunity to collaborate with a leading Pan-African feminist fund. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Discover more of these opportunities Here. The post African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SYPALA 2026: Applications Open for IMANI’s Premier Pan-African Summer Seminar
summary: The IMANI Center for Policy and Education has officially opened applications for the 12th edition of the Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA), a flagship programme dedicated to shaping the next generation of African policy thinkers and leaders. Scheduled as a four-day residential seminar, SYPALA 2026 continues its mission of equipping young Africans with the tools, networks, and intellectual exposure needed to advance economic resilience and inclusive governance across the continent. Since its inception, SYPALA has built a strong reputation as one of Africa s leading platforms for high-performing undergraduates and recent graduates eager to delve into public policy, governance, market-based reforms, and leadership development. The academy brings together bright, motivated participants from across the continent, offering an immersive environment where debate, critical thinking, and innovative problem-solving are encouraged. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the SYPALA 2026 Essay Requirement Acceptance into SYPALA 2026 is determined entirely by the strength and originality of an applicant s essay. This year s essay topic challenges young thinkers to engage deeply with Africa s policy and development landscape: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Identify one specific policy or business innovation that can significantly enhance economic resilience and foster inclusive governance in a specific African country. Analyse the current challenge, explain how your proposed solution works, and outline one major obstacle to its implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This prompt is crafted to assess applicants ability to think critically, diagnose real-world problems, and propose solutions with clear, actionable logic. The most competitive essays will demonstrate strong analytical depth, originality of thought, and a genuine interest in how policy or business innovations can advance economic opportunity and strengthen governance systems. In addition to the main essay, applicants must submit a summary of no more than 200 words , which will be used during the initial pre-selection process. Essays must be written in English, must not exceed 1200 words, and must be entirely original. Clear citation and proper referencing are required wherever external ideas or quotes are used. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Eligibility and Submission Requirements The SYPALA essay competition is open to undergraduate students and recent graduates from any African country. Applicants must include their full name, institution, current level of study, age, and contact information. All submissions must be made via email, using the designated addresses provided in the call for applications. Only original, unpublished work will be accepted. Essays suspected of plagiarism or AI-generated content without proper disclosure will be disqualified. The selection committee reviews entries on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Programme Benefits Applicants selected for SYPALA 2026 will receive a full scholarship , which covers accommodation, participation in all sessions, and access to a world-class faculty. Over the four days, participants will engage with thought-leaders, policy experts, and innovators working across governance, economics, entrepreneurship, and civic leadership. SYPALA s unique residential format fosters a learning environment that goes beyond formal lectures. Participants will take part in group discussions, simulation exercises, and networking sessions that encourage collaboration and peer-learning. By the end of the programme, attendees gain not only knowledge but a vibrant, pan-African network of like-minded young leaders committed to shaping the continent s future. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Why SYPALA Matters Africa s future depends on the strength, innovation, and ethical grounding of its next generation of leaders. SYPALA fills a crucial gap by nurturing young talent at a formative stage, equipping participants with the intellectual framework needed to understand policy challenges and envision practical solutions. Whether addressing economic instability, governance deficits, unemployment, or emerging opportunities in technology and entrepreneurship, SYPALA prepares young Africans to lead with clarity and confidence. Application Deadline Deadline: April 30, 2026 All essays must be submitted by the deadline to be considered. Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . The post SYPALA 2026: Applications Open for IMANI’s Premier Pan-African Summer Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship 2026–2028
summary: Are you a values-driven young leader with Muslim roots seeking to create meaningful global impact? The Azm Global Leaders (AGL) Fellowship 2026 2028 Cohort is now accepting screening applications for exceptional individuals ready to grow, lead, and serve with integrity. This highly competitive, two-year fellowship brings together a diverse global cohort committed to principled leadership, rigorous learning, and positive change across cultures and sectors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship The Azm Global Leaders Fellowship is designed for outstanding young professionals and changemakers who demonstrate academic or professional excellence, moral courage, and a strong commitment to service. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Fellows represent a wide range of countries, disciplines, and lived experiences united by shared values and a desire to lead with purpose. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply to AGL? The Azm Global Leaders Fellowship offers: A powerful global network of high-achieving peers Immersive in-person summits Cross-sector learning experiences Leadership development grounded in values A platform to amplify impact in your community and beyond If you are ready to lead with integrity and help shape a better world, the AGL Fellowship 2026 2028 Cohort could be your next transformative step. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the AGL Fellowship (2026 2028 Cohort), applicants must: Be 22 29 years old at the time of application Demonstrate English proficiency (B2 level or above) Hold a valid passport through July 2028 Be available to attend three mandatory two-week in-person summits (held during the first two weeks of July each year) Commit a minimum of 2 hours per week to learning throughout the two-year program ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); What the Selection Committee Looks For Meeting eligibility criteria is only the first step. The selection process is rigorous and holistic. Successful applicants typically demonstrate: 1. Academic or Professional Distinction A strong record of excellence and sustained achievement in a chosen field. 2. Mastery in a Specific Area Depth of expertise in a professional, academic, cultural, or creative discipline. 3. Integrity and Character Supported by two references from colleagues, mentors, or leaders who can speak to your values and conduct. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 4. Leadership Potential and Moral Courage A willingness to act on principle even when facing challenges. 5. Commitment to Service and Impact Proven engagement in community, academic, or professional leadership with a deep motivation to create positive change. Who Should Apply? AGL seeks individuals who: Act with purpose and intention Care deeply about their communities Ask thoughtful questions Show readiness for growth Value cross-cultural dialogue and global collaboration ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The fellowship aims to build a cohort that reflects the richness and diversity of the global Muslim community across regions, sectors, and perspectives. AGL Selection Process (2026 Cohort) The selection process unfolds in three stages: Stage 1: Application Deadline: March 15, 2026 Eligible applicants receive access to the full application, exploring experience, motivation, values, and leadership potential. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Stage 2: Interviews Dates: March 23 27, 2026 Shortlisted candidates will participate in a 45-minute panel interview . Stage 3: Offer Stage Notification: By April 1, 2026 Selected fellows receive formal invitations, contingent on references and supporting documents. Important Application Notes Before submitting your screening application , please note: Questions marked with (*) are required Applications can only be submitted once Answers cannot be edited after submission Final Deadline: March 15, 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Pro Tip for Applicants Be genuine. That s what stands out. Take your time, reflect deeply on your motivations, and answer honestly. The selection committee is not only looking for impressive achievements they are looking for principled individuals committed to growth and service. Application Deadline: March 15, 2026. Discover more fellowship opportunities here. The post Apply for the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship 2026–2028 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
